headline: Transcript: Are Trump’s tariffs legal? With Jennifer Hillman
summary: Alan Beattie talks to Jennifer Hillman
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of The Economics Show podcast episode: ‘Are Trump’s tariffs legal? With Jennifer Hillman’

Alan Beattie Tariffs or taxes on imports have been a powerful weapon in Donald Trump’s trade policy in his second term, but could they be illegal? The United States Supreme Court will next week start to hear a case that challenges the president’s right to impose those duties without the approval of Congress. Defeat for Trump could have major ramifications for US trade policy. Victory could give the president broad new powers, not just over tariffs. Which way is the case likely to go? And what will Trump’s options be if he loses?

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is The Economics Show. I’m Alan Beattie, the FT’s senior trade writer and author of the Trade Secrets newsletter. I’m joined today by Jennifer Hillman, professor at Georgetown University and a fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations.
